author: claude
content:
feat/sqlalchemy-postgresql-v2 #1161: RESOLVED (deep stack, 7 of 9 commits conflicted) — rebased -b 9 commits onto master 315e6672. ALL conflicts were environment.yaml (v0.35.0 pin churn vs the branch's evolving DB dependency story), resolved commit-by-commit (jj edit each conflicted commit, propagating downward) to preserve the stack's intent at each step:
- 79fb43da (SQLAlchemy rewrite): master pins + add conda sqlalchemy=2.0.36
- e8ff294e (pg test scenario): master pins + add postgresql=17.9, psycopg2=2.9.10, pytest-postgresql=7.0.1
- 6fc72da3 (HAS_PYTEST_POSTGRESQL): master pins, remove psycopg2 (per commit diff)
- 5f6cce0c (psycopg2 vs binary): master pins + psycopg2=2.9.11
- c473bfe3 (psycopg3): master pins, drop postgresql+psycopg2, add psycopg=3.3.3
- 2561fb3c (pip-install, 3-sided): master pins, move psycopg[binary]/pytest-postgresql/sqlalchemy OUT of conda INTO pip section (py3.14 compat)
- cf876d17 (task_id Integer): auto-resolved (no env change)
FINAL env.yaml verified: zero pg/sqlalchemy in conda deps; pip section has psycopg[binary]==3.3.3, pytest-postgresql==7.0.1, sqlalchemy==2.0.36. YAML valid.
VERIFY: jj-precommit FULLY GREEN — isort/black/mypy(ignore_missing_imports) + the check-environment-pins hook PASSED (confirms env.yaml matches the branch's unpinned sources). All 9 commits conflict-free; no markers anywhere; all 7 changed .py files py_compile clean.
ENV-BLOCKED at test runtime (NOT a regression): the openwpm conda env matches v0.35.0 manifest = NO sqlalchemy; the branch's root test/conftest.py imports SQLiteStorageProvider -> sqlalchemy_provider -> sqlalchemy, so pytest collection fails locally with ModuleNotFoundError. This is the branch's deliberately-added dependency, present in its environment.yaml/pip; CI installs env fresh and will have it. Same env-block class as FF152 browser tests / basedpyright (#1170). Cannot install sqlalchemy locally (do-not-modify-env). Lean on CI for runtime.
new tip cf876d17; MERGEABLE.
